source: funding_envelope.md — anchored to 2025 Ukraine Recovery Conference cycle. Re-verify before public rendering.

tranches.label: Pure grants
tranches.confidence_note: EU Facility Pillar I and named bilateral envelopes explicit in source documents.
tranches.direction_note: EU Facility Pillar I tapers post-2027; accession-linked top-ups partially offset.

tranches.label: ERA / frozen-asset proceeds
tranches.confidence_note: G7 ERA envelope explicit. Principal-seizure scenario is low-confidence additive.
tranches.direction_note: Principal seizure is G7 political decision; carries asymmetric tail. Default scenario: proceeds_only.

tranches.label: First-loss / guarantee capital
tranches.confidence_note: Aggregate guarantee envelope announced; deployment ratio inferred.
tranches.direction_note: Absolute capacity stable but declines in relative weight as commercial tranches grow.

tranches.label: Concessional IFI debt
tranches.confidence_note: World Bank / EBRD / EIB cumulative envelopes inferred from aggregate pledges.
tranches.direction_note: IFI Boards reconfirm post-war envelopes; EU Facility successor programme likely.

tranches.label: Senior IFI debt (near-market)
tranches.confidence_note: Inferred from EBRD / EIB near-market portfolio capacity.
tranches.direction_note: Revenue-generating assets become viable at scale post-armistice.

tranches.label: ECA buyer credit / direct lending
tranches.confidence_note: Named ECA envelopes explicit in programme documents.
tranches.direction_note: Pre-armistice: 30–50% utilisation of announced capacity. Post: caps lifted, bank syndication deepens.

tranches.label: PRI / war-risk wrap
tranches.direction_note: War premium falls; sovereign-risk residual remains. Less needed as commercial layers normalise.

tranches.label: DFI equity / quasi-equity
tranches.confidence_note: DFI equity allocations inferred from fund mandates.
tranches.direction_note: SOE restructurings and PPP concessions become viable post-armistice.

tranches.label: Sovereign / municipal counterpart equity
tranches.confidence_note: Wartime fiscal constraint limits pre-armistice contribution.
tranches.direction_note: Tax base rebuilds; oblast budgets expand as population returns.

tranches.label: Diaspora / patriotic bonds
tranches.confidence_note: Estimated from Israel/Ireland analogues and fund-manager soundings.
tranches.direction_note: Israel-style sustained diaspora bond programme becomes viable post-armistice.

tranches.label: Commercial bank senior debt
tranches.confidence_note: Estimated from A/B loan precedents; post-armistice is theoretical capacity.
tranches.direction_note: Pre-armistice: almost exclusively via IFI A/B structures. Post: standalone bank syndicates return.

tranches.label: Institutional / capital markets debt
tranches.confidence_note: Most policy-contingent tranche. EU accession stall compresses by 40–60%.
tranches.direction_note: Solvency II / matching-adjustment regimes activate as Ukraine sovereign rating crosses thresholds.

tranches.label: Private equity / infra equity funds
tranches.confidence_note: Estimated from fund-manager soundings and analogues.
tranches.direction_note: Global infra mandates, EM secondary, distressed/value funds mostly post-armistice.

conditionally_financeable: Energy BBB (depends on MIGA wrap + offtake structures)
Transport revenue-generating (depends on PPP concession standardisation)
Housing BBB (depends on EU Housing Pillar envelope)
Water/sanitation BBB (depends on PPP municipal capacity)
capacity_constrained: Industrial assets (steel, coke, agri-processing) — pre-armistice gap ~USD 50–60bn
Heritage in formerly-occupied areas — absorptive-capacity constraint
Frontline-adjacent residential — rebuildability filter excludes most deployment
Industrial in re-damaged zones (Mariupol, Avdiivka) — capacity collapses
Smaller-scale municipal infrastructure — transaction-cost economics fail
